title: hieracium laniferum subsp. aragonense
intro: Hieracium laniferum subsp. aragonense, also known as woolly hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium laniferum subsp. aragonense include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve skin conditions such as acne and eczema, and alleviate digestive issues like diarrhea and constipation. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs for enhanced therapeutic effects.
